title: ficus neriifolia var. trilepis
intro: Ficus neriifolia var. trilepis, commonly known as the Chinese banyan or three-leaved fig, is a plant species native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its broad, three-lobed leaves. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Ficus neriifolia var. trilepis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
